author: Dr. Patricia Lee
content:
TELEPHONE ENCOUNTER — Prescription Refill Request and Imaging Results

Patient: Robert Morrison, 72M
Call initiated by: Patient via pharmacy callback request
Spoke with: Patient

Reason for Call:
1. Pharmacy requested authorization for refill of gabapentin 300mg TID — prescription expired.
2. Patient also asking about recent lab results from June 2024 visit.
3. Incidental discussion of upcoming imaging.

Medication Review:
Gabapentin 300mg TID for diabetic peripheral neuropathy. Patient reports stable pain control at 4/10. No side effects (no excessive sedation, no dizziness, no peripheral edema). Has been on this dose since June 2020. Refill authorized for 90-day supply with 3 refills.

Lab Results Reviewed (drawn 6/20/2024):
- BMP: Na 136, K 4.7, Cl 100, CO2 24 (improved from 22 with sodium bicarbonate), BUN 36, Cr 1.93 (eGFR 36.5), Glucose 138
- CBC: WBC 7.1, Hgb 11.0, Hct 33.0%, Plt 188
- HbA1c: 7.4% (stable, good control)
- BNP: 450 (stable)
- Potassium 4.7 — acceptable but on the higher side given Entresto + spironolactone + empagliflozin. Patient counseled to continue monitoring.

Discussion:
1. Kidney function stable — eGFR 36.5, unchanged from prior. Patient reassured.
2. A1c 7.4% — maintaining good control since starting empagliflozin in May. Patient has been adherent to diet and medications.
3. Discussed empagliflozin — patient reports tolerating well, no genital infections or UTIs. Mildly increased urination but manageable. Encouraged adequate hydration within his 1.5L fluid restriction.
4. Patient reports his wife Patricia is concerned about his mood — he has been less interested in going to church and watching grandchildren. He acknowledges this but attributes it to fatigue. Encouraged him to discuss this with his PCP at next visit. Suggested that improved physical activity (walking program) might help both mood and HF symptoms.
5. Discussed upcoming imaging: CT abdomen/pelvis ordered by PCP for abdominal discomfort evaluation (scheduled September), and TEE ordered by cardiology for comprehensive cardiac assessment.
6. Patient asked about his empagliflozin and whether it could be causing his increased urination. Explained that mild increase in urination is expected and is part of the mechanism of action. Confirmed it is separate from his BPH-related symptoms.

Plan:
- Gabapentin 300mg TID refilled — 90-day supply with 3 refills
- Continue all current medications
- Encourage discussion of mood changes with PCP
- CT abdomen/pelvis and TEE as scheduled
- Next endocrinology follow-up August 2024
- Next nephrology follow-up as scheduled
- Call back with any concerns

Patient verbalized understanding of all results and plan.
attestation:
Telephone encounter documented. Prescription refill authorized. Lab results reviewed with patient.

Electronically signed by: Dr. Patricia Lee, MD
Date: 6/26/2024
